description: This set of images presents TEM, HRTEM and selected area electron diffraction characterization of Zr0.50 steel (Fe-25Mn-7Al-0.9C-0.50Zr low-density steel) after tensile deformation. Subfigure a shows planar slip and dislocations inside FCC grains after minor deformation, with the SAED pattern calibrated as <110> FCC orientation. Subfigure b reveals dense slip bands in the heavily deformed FCC matrix, with its SAED also indexed as <110> FCC. Subfigure c displays high-density nanotwin regions, with SAED containing both <110> FCC and <110> twin diffraction spots. Subfigure c1 is an HRTEM image that clearly shows the coherent interface between nanoscale twin and FCC matrix. Subfigure d presents the interaction between mobile dislocations and ~30 nm ZrC precipitated particles, while subfigure d1 confirms these particles are ZrC with <110> orientation via FFT pattern and HRTEM lattice imaging. These results demonstrate multiple cooperative deformation mechanisms including dislocation slip, twinning-induced plasticity, and ZrC precipitation strengthening, which jointly contribute to the excellent strength-ductility synergy of the Zr-alloyed low-density steel.
